schema.appDescription: Calculate simple interest for any principal, rate, and time period. See interest earned and total maturity amount instantly.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Simple Interest Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option for your situation.

faq.q: How accurate is the Simple Interest Calculator?
faq.a: The Simple Interest Calculator uses standard financial formulas. Results are indicative; actual figures depend on your lender or financial institution's policies.

faq.q: Is the Simple Interest Calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, the Simple Interest Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations happen in your browser. We do not store any of your financial data.

faq.q: How often are the Simple Interest Calculator rates updated?
faq.a: Rates in examples are updated periodically to reflect current market conditions. Always verify with your bank or advisor.

faq.q: Can I use the Simple Interest Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised. The calculator works on all modern smartphones and tablets.
Anyone in India planning their finances can benefit from the Simple Interest Calculator. It's designed for both beginners and experienced investors.

examples.heading: ₹5 lakh investment at 10% for 10 years
examples.text: ₹5 lakh invested at 10% p.a. grows to ₹12.97 lakh in 10 years through compounding. Simple interest would yield only ₹10 lakh — demonstrating the power of compounding over long periods.

examples.heading: ₹1 lakh at 12% CAGR over 20 years
examples.text: ₹1 lakh growing at 12% p.a. becomes ₹9.65 lakh in 20 years. Even a 2% difference (at 14%) produces ₹13.74 lakh — demonstrating how small return differences compound dramatically.
